item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't feel like I'm a part of the two crowds of people that would enjoy this type of article: those who have lost a significant other, or those that just love animals so much that they're happy enough with the inclusion of the chicken. Because of that, this feels extremely generic and doesn't invoke much on the topic of losing ones friends and family besides the occasional "I wish Marguerit was here" which doesn't have any impact since there are only three lines of lackluster characterization to her name.

This is a -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this skip on a reddit post you made a while back. To be perfectly honest, this is one of my favorite SCPs. Absolutely love the story being told here, +10 if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I'm getting some Series 1 vibes here in a good way. I really appreciate the simplicity and the story that goes with it. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A nice mix of wholesomeness and sadness that I don't really see often from this site. The story with the farmer really puts everything together and adds a great emotional connection to a otherwise simple animated chicken statue.

Great work +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is lovely and sad at once. I love short and poignant skips like this. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so seeing Alan Tudyk's portrayal of Heihei the idiot chicken in the movie Moana and playing too much Stardew Valley has given me a psychic weakness to chickens, so this is a +1 from me. Short, sweet, and meaningful in the Foundation-verse because this thing belonged to someone and was special to that someone.

buk buk buk :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first mainlist SCP - hope you like it!

Thanks to Galaxy-Class, Zyn, and Drblackbox for providing feedback during the drafting process! Sorry if not all of it could be incorporated, but I'm happy with how the article turned out.

Sculpture by Wilhelm Schimmel, image by the Metropolitan Museum of Art, released to the Public Domain under CC0.
https://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search/12537
Cropped and colorized by me.
